她.origin: The woman radical 女 plus 也 — it means 'she,' the girl standing right beside you.

sentence.en: She is my good friend.

服.origin: Picture a person slipping their arm 又 into a coat 月 — 服 means clothes.

sentence.en: Today I am wearing new clothes.

干.origin: A simple shape like a wooden pole standing straight up; 干 means dry or to do.

sentence.en: The clothes are dried very dry.

呢.origin: A mouth 口 next to 尼 — say 'ne' out loud, the little word you add when asking.

sentence.en: Where is your school bag?

办.origin: Strength 力 in the middle with two dots beside it — use your power 力 to get things done.

sentence.en: I will take care of this matter.

片.origin: Half of a tree trunk split down the middle — 片 means a thin flat slice.

sentence.en: I ate a slice of bread.

兔.origin: Look closely and you can see a rabbit with long ears and a tiny tail dot 丶.

sentence.en: The little rabbit loves to eat carrots.

赛.origin: Under a roof 宀 sits a shell 贝 — long ago shells were money, the prize you win in a 赛, a race.

sentence.en: There is a running race tomorrow.

乌.origin: Like the bird 鸟 but missing the eye dot — a crow is so black you cannot even see its eye!

sentence.en: There is a dark cloud in the sky.

步.origin: Two feet 止, one in front of the other — 步 is taking a step as you walk.

sentence.en: Let's go for a walk together.

爬.origin: A claw 爪 reaching and grabbing — 爬 means to climb up, hand over hand.

sentence.en: The kitten climbed up the tree.

慢.origin: The heart radical 忄 on the left — a calm, easy heart takes its time, so 慢 means slow.

sentence.en: He walks very slowly.

追.origin: The walking radical 辶 — your feet keep moving fast to 追, to chase after someone.

sentence.en: The puppy runs chasing the ball.

仍.origin: A person 亻 standing beside 乃 — they are still there, so 仍 means still or yet.

sentence.en: It is still raining outside.

第.origin: Bamboo strips ⺮ laid out in order, with 弟 below — 第 marks the order, like 'number one.'

sentence.en: He won first place.

样.origin: A tree 木 beside a sheep 羊 — 样 means a shape, kind, or pattern of things.

sentence.en: Our clothes are the same.

终.origin: The thread radical 纟 with 冬 (winter) — winter is the end of the year, so 终 means end.

sentence.en: We finally got home.

钓.origin: The metal radical 钅 makes a little hook 勺 — 钓 is fishing with a hook and line.

sentence.en: Grandpa is fishing by the river.

只.origin: A mouth 口 with two little strokes 八 — just a tiny word, so 只 means only or just.

sentence.en: I only have one pencil.

捉.origin: The hand radical 扌 beside a foot 足 — chase with your feet, then grab with your hand to 捉, catch.

sentence.en: The cat wants to catch that mouse.

什.origin: A person 亻 next to ten 十 — when there are ten of something mixed up, you ask 什, 'what?'

sentence.en: What are you doing?

么.origin: A tiny curling shape — 么 is a little ending sound, used in the question word 什么.

sentence.en: How do you come to school?

也.origin: A single curvy stroke 乚 — easy to write and easy to say, 也 means 'also' or 'too.'

sentence.en: I also want to go to the park.

几.origin: Two little legs holding up a flat top — 几 looks like a small table, and also asks 'how many?'

sentence.en: How many people are in your family?

专.origin: A simple shape that turns and focuses — 专 means to concentrate on one special thing.

sentence.en: In class you must listen attentively.

笑.origin: Bamboo ⺮ on top, a swaying person 夭 below — picture a face crinkling up; 笑 means to laugh.

sentence.en: Little sister laughs very happily.

狼.origin: The dog radical 犭 on the left — a wolf is a wild cousin of the dog, so 狼 means wolf.

sentence.en: There is a wolf in the forest.

活.origin: Water 氵 plus a tongue 舌 — water keeps things alive, so 活 means to live.

sentence.en: The little fish lives in the water.

弄.origin: Two hands 廾 holding up a piece of jade 玉 — playing with it; 弄 means to do or fiddle with.

sentence.en: Don't get your clothes dirty.

声.origin: Think of something making noise that reaches your ear — 声 means sound or voice.

sentence.en: I hear the sound of birds.

喊.origin: The mouth radical 口 on the left — open your mouth wide and 喊, shout out loud!

sentence.en: He loudly shouts my name.

刻.origin: The knife radical 刂 on the right — use a blade to 刻, to carve marks into wood or stone.

sentence.en: It is now a quarter past six.

跟.origin: The foot radical 足 on the left — your feet 跟 follow along behind someone.

sentence.en: The duckling follows its mother.

撒.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 散 (scatter) — toss seeds with your hand; 撒 means to scatter or let go.

sentence.en: He scattered a handful of rice.

谎.origin: The speech radical 讠 plus 荒 — words that are empty and wild; 谎 means a lie.

sentence.en: A good child does not lie.

欺.origin: The right side 欠 shows an open mouth — tricky words used to 欺, to cheat someone.

sentence.en: We do not bully our classmates.

骗.origin: The horse radical 马 on the left — once it meant jumping sideways onto a horse; now 骗 means to cheat.

sentence.en: He uses lies to deceive people.

理.origin: The jade radical 王 plus 里 — carefully sorting the lines in jade; 理 means reason or to tidy.

sentence.en: What mother says makes a lot of sense.

静.origin: On the left is 青 (green calm) — like a quiet green field, 静 means still and quiet.

sentence.en: Everyone please be a little quieter.

夜.origin: A person 亻 tucked under a cover 亠 with the moon-evening 夕 — 夜 means nighttime.

sentence.en: At night there are stars in the sky.
